excerpt: He removed his own shawl from his shoulders and draped it across her
source_location_hint: hadith of the Prophet's compassion to Fatima

excerpt: Fatima is the foremost of the women of Paradise
source_work: Sahih al-Tirmidhi, Fadail al-Nissa (Chapter on the Virtues of Women)

excerpt: Abraham consulted his son about the dream, asking his counsel rather than commanding
Ishmael replied with patience and submission to the divine command
This was indeed the manifest trial; We ransomed him with a mighty sacrifice
Complaint to Allah in Taif: O Allah, I complain to You of my weakness and humiliation
The Prophet wept at his mother's grave, heedless of heat and sun
Umm Ayman found the Prophet clinging to his mother's grave and comforted him
source_location_hint: hadith on the Prophet's devotion to his mother

excerpt: Let the heart be revived by admonition and illumined by wisdom; sadden it through the changing signs of time
source_location_hint: ethical counsel on contemplation of death

excerpt: Walk among their dwellings and consider their ruins; they departed from the house of deception
source_location_hint: exhortation on the transience of worldly life

excerpt: Do not sell your Hereafter for this world
source_location_hint: command concerning the choice between worldly and eternal life

excerpt: Girl's verses on divine love and longing at the Kaaba, understood by Junayd Baghdadi
source_location_hint: story of a woman saint's mystical poetry

excerpt: Piety has drawn me here; His love has rendered me mad with longing
source_location_hint: dialogue between mystic woman and Junayd

excerpt: Those who circle the Kaaba have hearts harder than stone; true lovers lose themselves in love alone
source_location_hint: critique of outward ritual without inward sincerity

excerpt: All creation declares: He is my Beloved, my Beloved
source_location_hint: mystical aphorism on creation's witness to divine unity

excerpt: The cloud over the Prophet's head in Syria recognized his station; the tree's shade followed him
source_location_hint: account of Bahaira the monk's recognition of Muhammad

excerpt: The deer asked permission to return to her young; the Prophet released her on trust
source_location_hint: story illustrating divine trust and the Prophet's compassion

excerpt: Go in peace to your family; truth delivers, falsehood destroys
source_location_hint: concluding wisdom on the supremacy of truth
